text:
Once upon a time in a quiet little forest, there was a wise old owl named Olga. She loved to read stories to the young birds. One crisp autumn day, Olga decided to tell the tale of a courageous squirrel.

She began:

"In a forest where leaves were golden and birds sang silvery tunes, there lived a tiny squirrel named Oliver.

Each day, Oliver filled his sack with nuts. But there was a wise old badger who enjoyed collecting nuts.

One afternoon, as Oliver gathered ripe pecans, the badger sneaked up. With a quick flick of his muddy paw, he stole all Oliver's nuts.

Poor Oliver! Without his feast. But oh!

He talked to the moon. "Moon, I dare you to get those nuts back!"

The moon flicked its big white ring. "Just wait."

That night, a big eagle swooped down. "You little nutria!"

With a flap of wings, your feathers shine!

The eagle let the nuts
